name: 5-Amino-1MQ
notes: NNMT inhibitor for fat loss and metabolic enhancement. Oral capsules 50-100 mg 1-2x/day. Cycle 4-8 weeks. May support weight loss, muscle preservation, and energy. Limited human clinical data; based on research and community protocols.

name: ACE-031 (ACVR2B-Fc)
notes: Myostatin inhibitor fusion protein (ActRIIB decoy receptor). Clinical trial doses in muscular dystrophy were 1-3 mg/kg. Caused nosebleeds and gum bleeding in trials due to off-target activin effects. Very limited safe human dosing data.

name: AICAR
notes: 50 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~16.7 mg/mL. WADA-prohibited in sport. Monitor kidney function. Cycle no longer than 14 days; 1–2 month washout before repeating. Research use only.

name: AOD-9604
frequency: Once daily, in the morning on empty stomach
notes: Fragment of HGH (amino acids 176-191) modified for fat loss. Mimics lipolytic effects without affecting IGF-1 or blood glucose. Take 30 minutes before food or exercise. Cycle 3-6 months.

name: ARA-290
notes: Innate Repair Receptor agonist derived from EPO. Used for neuropathic pain, sarcoidosis, and metabolic syndrome. Clinical trials: 4 mg SC daily for 28 days. Anti-inflammatory and tissue-protective without erythropoietic effects.

name: Abaloparatide
notes: FDA-approved PTHrP analogue (Tymlos) for postmenopausal osteoporosis. Standard dose: 80 mcg/day SC into periumbilical abdomen. Maximum treatment duration: 2 years. Similar mechanism to teriparatide. Rotate injection sites within abdominal area.

name: Adamax
notes: Gradual titration recommended; 10 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~3.33 mg/mL. Adamantane-modified Semax analog studied for nootropic/neuroprotective properties. Research use only.

name: Adipotide
dosage: 250 mcg escalating by 250 mcg every 2 weeks
notes: Starting dose 250 mcg; 10 mg vial reconstituted to ~3.33 mg/mL. Clinical development discontinued due to reversible kidney toxicity at higher doses. Research use only.

name: Angiotensin 1-7
frequency: Continuous infusion or multiple daily injections
notes: Counter-regulatory arm of renin-angiotensin system. Cardioprotective, vasodilatory, and anti-fibrotic. Cyclic form (cAng 1-7) has oral bioavailability. Research and clinical trial stage. Doses extrapolated from animal studies.

name: BPC-157
route: Subcutaneous or intramuscular injection; oral capsules also used for gut issues
notes: Commonly used for healing injuries, gut health, and inflammation. Inject near site of injury when possible. Typical cycle is 4-6 weeks. Oral dosing ranges 500-1000 mcg/day for GI conditions.

name: Bremelanotide
frequency: As needed, 45 minutes before sexual activity
notes: FDA-approved (Vyleesi) for hypoactive sexual desire disorder in premenopausal women. Max 1 dose per 24 hours, max 1x per week recommended. Common side effects: nausea, flushing, headache. Consider antiemetic pretreatment.

name: Bremelanotide (PT-141)
frequency: As needed, no more than once per 24 hours and no more than 1x/week
notes: FDA-approved for hypoactive sexual desire disorder in women at 1.75 mg. Administer 45 minutes before sexual activity. Off-label male use: 0.5-2 mg. Nausea and flushing common; antiemetic pretreatment may help. Transient blood pressure increase expected.

name: CJC-1295
notes: Non-DAC version (without Drug Affinity Complex). Short half-life (~30 min). Often combined with Ipamorelin in a 1:1 ratio. Stimulates GH pulse mimicking natural release. Cycle 8-12 weeks.

name: CJC-1295 DAC
notes: DAC version has extended half-life of 6-8 days due to Drug Affinity Complex. Causes sustained GH elevation rather than pulsatile release. Some prefer non-DAC for more natural GH patterns. Cycle 8-12 weeks.

name: Cagrilintide
notes: Gradual titration over 4–6 weeks from 0.25 mg/week up to 4.5 mg/week (or 2.4 mg/week in CagriSema combination protocols). Long-acting amylin analogue. GI side effects common during titration. Research use only.

name: Cartalax
notes: 20 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~6.67 mg/mL. Khavinson bioregulator tripeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp). Typically used in 10–20 day courses; repeat every 4–6 months. Limited human clinical data. Research use only.

name: Cerebrolysin
notes: 60 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields 20 mg/mL. Doses exceeding 100 units (1.0 mL) should be split into AM and PM administrations. Porcine brain-derived neuropeptide mixture. Research use only.

name: Chonluten
notes: 20 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields 6.67 mg/mL. Gradual titration over 8–16 weeks. Bioregulatory tripeptide (Glu-Asp-Gly) studied for bronchopulmonary tissue support. Research use only.

name: Colivelin
notes: Neuroprotective peptide combining ADNF and humanin. Extremely potent; active at very low doses (nanomolar range). Used for Alzheimer's, neurodegeneration, and cognitive enhancement. Very limited human data; dosing largely based on animal studies.

name: Cortagen
notes: 20 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~6.67 mg/mL. Khavinson bioregulator tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Pro) studied for neuroprotection and nerve regeneration. Research use only.

name: DSIP
notes: Delta sleep-inducing peptide. Promotes slow-wave sleep and has stress-reducing properties. Low-dose 100 mcg for sleep support; higher doses up to 500 mcg for more pronounced effects. Cycle 2-4 weeks.

name: Desmopressin
dosage: 0.1-0.4 mg oral; 10-40 mcg intranasal; 1-4 mcg injectable
frequency: Once daily at bedtime or as directed
notes: Synthetic ADH analogue. FDA-approved for diabetes insipidus, nocturia, and hemophilia A. Oral: 0.1-0.4 mg/day. Intranasal: 10-40 mcg. Injectable: 1-4 mcg/day. Monitor for hyponatremia. Limit fluid intake.

name: Desmopressin (DDAVP)
dosage: Intranasal: 10-40 mcg; Oral: 0.1-0.4 mg; Injectable: 2-4 mcg
frequency: Once or twice daily depending on indication
notes: Synthetic vasopressin analogue. Used for diabetes insipidus, nocturnal enuresis, and hemophilia. Cognitive/nootropic off-label use at 2.5-10 mcg intranasally. Risk of hyponatremia; limit fluid intake after dosing.

name: Dihexa
notes: Potent nootropic and cognitive enhancer acting on HGF/MET pathway. Oral doses 10-20 mg; transdermal application also effective. Extremely potent; reportedly 7 orders of magnitude more potent than BDNF. Use caution; limited human safety data.

name: Epitalon
route: Subcutaneous or intramuscular injection; intranasal also used
notes: Telomerase activator and anti-aging peptide. Typical protocol: 5-10 mg/day for 10-20 days, repeated 2x per year. Intranasal: 15-30 mcg/day. Studied for longevity, pineal gland function, and immune modulation.

name: Epithalon
route: Subcutaneous or intramuscular injection; intranasal also used
notes: Same as Epitalon (alternate spelling/name). Tetrapeptide Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly. Protocol: 5-10 mg/day for 10-20 consecutive days, 2 courses per year. Associated with telomere elongation and anti-aging effects.

name: FOXO4-DRI
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~3.33 mg/mL. Gradual titration over 16 weeks. Senolytic peptide; no human clinical data exist. Preclinical evidence only from mouse studies. Research use only.

name: Follistatin 344
notes: Myostatin and activin inhibitor promoting muscle growth. Typical cycle: 50-100 mcg/day for 10-30 days. Cycle no more than 1-2 times per year due to potential long-term effects on reproductive hormones and tissue overgrowth risk.

name: Fragment 176-191
frequency: Once or twice daily, on empty stomach
notes: C-terminal fragment of HGH for fat loss. Administer in fasted state (morning or before exercise) for best results. Does not affect blood glucose or IGF-1 levels. Cycle 3-6 months. Often combined with CJC-1295/Ipamorelin.

name: Fragment 176-191 (HGH Frag)
frequency: 2-3 times daily; optimal timing: fasted morning, pre-workout, pre-sleep
notes: C-terminal fragment of HGH targeting fat metabolism without IGF-1 mediated growth effects. Best administered in fasted state. Avoid carbohydrates around dosing window. Typical cycle 8-12 weeks. No effect on blood glucose unlike full HGH.

name: GHK-Cu
dosage: 1-2 mg per injection or topical cream
frequency: Once daily for injectable; topical applied 1-2x daily
notes: Copper peptide with regenerative, anti-aging, and wound-healing properties. Injectable: 1-2 mg/day SC. Topical formulations widely available for skin. Promotes collagen, elastin, and tissue remodeling. Cycle 4-8 weeks.

name: GHRP-2
notes: Potent GH secretagogue. Increases cortisol and prolactin more than Ipamorelin. Strong appetite stimulation. Take on empty stomach. Commonly combined with a GHRH peptide for synergy. Cycle 8-12 weeks.

name: GHRP-6
notes: Causes significant hunger/appetite stimulation via ghrelin pathway. Useful for those looking to increase appetite and muscle mass. Some cortisol and prolactin elevation. Take on empty stomach. Cycle 8-12 weeks.

name: Glutathione
notes: 600 mg vial reconstituted with 2.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields 300 mg/mL. Cycle 4–8 weeks; optional 2–4 week off-cycle. Relatively unstable once reconstituted—use within 2–3 weeks. Research use only.

name: Gonadorelin
notes: 2 mg vial reconstituted with 2.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields 1 mg/mL. Synthetic GnRH; stimulates LH/FSH pulsatile release to restore HPG axis. Used alongside TRT to maintain testicular function. Research use only.

name: HCG
notes: Standard maintenance: 500 IU per injection (1,500 IU/week); high-dose recovery: 1,500–2,500 IU per injection. 5000 IU vial reconstituted with 2.0 mL bacteriostatic water. Protocol duration 8–16 weeks. Monitor testosterone and estradiol. Research use only.

name: HGH 191AA
notes: 10 IU vial reconstituted with 1 mL bacteriostatic water yields 10 IU/mL. Monitor fasting glucose at doses ≥2 IU/day. WADA-prohibited in sport. Store lyophilized at −20°C; refrigerate reconstituted at 2–8°C for up to 14 days. Research use only.

name: HMG
notes: 75 IU (0.15 mg) vial typically reconstituted in 1 mL diluent for 75 IU/mL. Contains 1:1 blend of FSH and LH. Used alongside HCG for spermatogenesis support. Medical supervision required. Research use only.

name: Hexarelin
notes: Most potent GHRP. Associated with desensitization with prolonged use; cycle 4-6 weeks on, 4 weeks off. Elevates cortisol and prolactin. Also has cardioprotective properties. Take on empty stomach.

name: Humanin
notes: Mitochondrial-derived peptide with neuroprotective and metabolic benefits. Research doses: 2-4 mg SC 3x/week. Associated with IGF-1 pathway. Anti-apoptotic and cytoprotective properties. Limited human clinical data.

name: IGF-1 DES
notes: Truncated IGF-1 analogue ~10x more potent than IGF-1 LR3. Very short half-life (~20-30 min). Inject intramuscularly into target muscle for localized effects. Start at 50 mcg; max 150 mcg/injection. Cycle 4 weeks maximum.

name: IGF-1 DES (Des(1-3)IGF-1)
frequency: Once daily or split twice daily
notes: Truncated IGF-1 with 10x higher potency than standard IGF-1 LR3. Does not bind IGF binding proteins. Very short half-life (20-30 min). Inject into target muscle for localized effects. Risk of hypoglycemia; have glucose source available. Cycle 4-6 weeks.

name: IGF-1 LR3
notes: Long-acting IGF-1 analogue with ~20-hour half-life. Start at 20-40 mcg/day and assess response. Cycle maximum 4-6 weeks due to receptor desensitization. Risk of hypoglycemia; take with food. Used for muscle growth and recovery.

name: IGF-2 (Insulin-like Growth Factor 2)
notes: Acts on IGF-1R and IGF-2R; important in muscle memory and satellite cell activation. May enhance recovery of previously trained muscle. Less hypoglycemic risk than IGF-1 DES. Limited human dosing data. Research peptide status.

name: Ipamorelin
frequency: 2-3 times daily or once at bedtime
notes: Selective GHRP with minimal cortisol/prolactin release. Commonly stacked with CJC-1295 or Mod GRF 1-29 for synergistic GH release. Take on empty stomach for best results. Cycle 8-12 weeks.

name: KPV
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~3.33 mg/mL. Anti-inflammatory tripeptide (C-terminal fragment of alpha-MSH). Generally well tolerated; no melanotropic effects. Research use only.

name: Kisspeptin
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~3.33 mg/mL. Upstream trigger of the GnRH/LH/FSH reproductive hormone cascade via GPR54 receptor. Studied for IVF/ovulation induction. Tachyphylaxis risk with prolonged continuous exposure. Research use only.

name: Kisspeptin-10
notes: Stimulates GnRH release and downstream LH/FSH/testosterone. Off-label use for hypogonadism and fertility. Doses 50-100 mcg SC. Clinical studies use IV. Emerging use in reproductive medicine. Limited human data on optimal protocols.

name: Kisspeptin-54
dosage: 1 nmol/kg for IV bolus; 0.1-1 mcg/kg for subcutaneous
frequency: Varies by protocol; typically pulsatile every 90 minutes in research settings
notes: GnRH secretagogue used in fertility research and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism. Pulsatile administration mimics physiological signaling. Continuous infusion leads to desensitization. Limited clinical data; primarily investigational.

name: Klotho (Klotho Peptide Fragment)
notes: Anti-aging protein associated with longevity, cognitive function, and kidney protection. Extremely limited human data. Most data from animal studies. Handle with care; cold-chain storage required.

name: L-Carnitine
notes: 200 mg vial reconstituted with 2.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields 100 mg/mL. Subcutaneous route bypasses gut bacterial TMAO production associated with oral dosing. Supports fat oxidation and exercise performance. Research use only.

name: LL-37
notes: 5 mg vial reconstituted to ~1.67 mg/mL. Endogenous human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide studied for immune modulation and wound healing. Research use only.

name: Larazotide
notes: Tight junction regulator (zonulin antagonist) for leaky gut and celiac disease. Clinical trials used 0.5-2 mg three times daily before meals. Reduces intestinal permeability. Off-label compounded use for gut health and autoimmune conditions.

name: Larazotide (AT-1001)
frequency: Three times daily, 30 minutes before meals
notes: Tight junction regulator used for leaky gut and celiac disease management. Take on empty stomach before meals. Clinical trials used 0.5 mg three times daily. May help with intestinal permeability disorders.

name: Livagen
dosage: 500 mcg–2 mg (standard); up to 10 mg for intensive 10-day courses
notes: 20 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~6.67 mg/mL. Cycle 2–8 weeks standard; intensive reset 10–14 days. Khavinson tetrapeptide bioregulator studied for epigenetic modulation and enkephalin-degrading enzyme inhibition. Research use only.

name: MGF
notes: 5 mg vial. Mechano growth factor (IGF-1 splice variant). Inject near target muscle post-training for localized effects. Short half-life (~minutes); PEG-MGF preferred for systemic/longer-acting protocols. Research use only.

name: MK-677
notes: Oral ghrelin mimetic / GH secretagogue. Not a peptide injection. Start at 10 mg/day, increase to 25 mg if tolerated. Common side effects: water retention, increased appetite, fatigue initially. Cycle 3-6 months.

name: MOTS-C
notes: Gradual titration over ~10 weeks. Multiple vial sizes (5, 10, 20, 40 mg). Mitochondria-derived peptide that activates AMPK pathways. No completed human clinical trials. Use reconstituted solution within 7 days for best potency. Research use only.

name: Mazdutide
dosage: 2.5–6 mg (5 mg vial) or 2.5–5 mg (10 mg vial)
notes: Gradual titration required. 5 mg vial: reconstitute with 3.0 mL → 1.67 mg/mL; 10 mg vial: reconstitute with 3.0 mL → 3.33 mg/mL. Dual GLP-1/glucagon receptor agonist. Approved in China for obesity; investigational elsewhere. Research use only.

name: Mechano Growth Factor (MGF)
notes: Splice variant of IGF-1 released in response to mechanical stress. Very short half-life (minutes); PEG-MGF has extended half-life. Inject into trained muscle within 30 minutes post-exercise. Typically cycled 4-8 weeks. Often stacked with IGF-1 LR3 sequentially.

name: Melanotan I (Afamelanotide)
dosage: 16 mg implant or 0.5-1 mg injectable per day
frequency: Implant every 2 months; Injectable daily for 10 days then as needed
notes: Non-selective melanocortin agonist for skin tanning and photoprotection. Licensed as implant (Scenesse) for erythropoietic protoporphyria. Less sexual side effects than Melanotan II. Nausea, flushing, and darkening of existing moles reported.

name: Melanotan II
dosage: 250 mcg loading, titrating to ~1000 mcg; 250–500 mcg maintenance
frequency: Daily during loading (4–8 weeks); 2–3 times weekly for maintenance
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL → ~3.33 mg/mL. Non-selective melanocortin receptor agonist; promotes skin tanning, sexual arousal, appetite suppression. Side effects include nausea, facial flushing, spontaneous erections. Research use only.

name: Mod GRF 1-29
frequency: 2-3 times daily or once at bedtime
notes: Modified CJC-1295 without DAC. Stimulates natural pulsatile GH release. Half-life ~30 minutes. Typically paired with a GHRP (Ipamorelin, GHRP-2, etc.) in a 1:1 ratio. Administer on empty stomach. Cycle 8-12 weeks.

name: Mod GRF 1-29 (CJC-1295 without DAC)
frequency: 2-3 times daily; often combined with a GHRP
notes: Modified GHRH analogue with approximately 30-minute half-life. Synergistic with GHRPs (GHRP-2, GHRP-6, Ipamorelin). Best dosed fasted and before sleep. Typical stacking dose 100 mcg Mod GRF + 100 mcg GHRP. Cycle 3-6 months on, 1-2 months off.

name: N-Acetyl Epithalon Amidate
frequency: Once daily for 10-20 consecutive days, 2 times per year
notes: Acetylated and amidated form of Epithalon (Epitalon) for enhanced stability and bioavailability. Telomerase activator with proposed anti-aging effects. More potent than standard Epithalon on molar basis.

name: NAD+
notes: Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide. IV infusions at 250-1000 mg over several hours. Subcutaneous 25-50 mg/day as maintenance. Used for energy, anti-aging, addiction recovery. IV may cause chest tightness; slow infusion rate.

name: Ovagen
dosage: Per protocol (typically 1–2 mg daily based on Khavinson bioregulator class)
notes: 20 mg vial. Ovarian tissue-derived bioregulatory peptide from the Khavinson class. Used to support female reproductive/ovarian function. Specific dosage data from peptidedosages.com protocol page not publicly indexed; dose extrapolated from bioregulator class norms. Research use only.

name: Oxyntomodulin
notes: Dual GLP-1/glucagon agonist for weight loss. Clinical research doses: 400-800 mcg SC before meals. Reduces appetite and increases energy expenditure. Not commercially available; research/compounded only. Limited long-term data.

name: Oxytocin
notes: 5 mg and 10 mg vials available on peptidedosages.com. Intranasal: typically 24–32 IU daily in clinical studies. Subcutaneous research protocols follow reconstituted vial approach. Social bonding/anxiolytic peptide. Research use only.

name: P21 Peptide
notes: Nootropic peptide targeting BDNF pathways. May support neurogenesis and synaptic plasticity. Very limited human data. Intranasal route preferred for CNS penetration. Cycle on for 30 days, off for 30 days.

name: PACAP (Pituitary Adenylate Cyclase-Activating Peptide)
frequency: Once daily or every other day
notes: Neuroprotective peptide related to VIP. May support cognitive function, neural repair, and metabolic regulation. Limited human dosing data. Headache reported at higher doses. Start low and titrate.

name: PDA
notes: Proline-rich Decapeptide Analogue. Healing and anti-inflammatory peptide. Often used similarly to BPC-157 for gut and injury repair. Typical dose 200-400 mcg once or twice daily. Limited published human data; dosing based on community protocols.

name: PE-22-28
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted to 3.33 mg/mL. Spadin analog; TREK-1 potassium channel blocker studied for rapid-onset antidepressant and neurogenic effects. Limited human data; research stage compound. Research use only.

name: PEG MGF
notes: 2 mg vial reconstituted to ~0.667 mg/mL. Pegylated mechano growth factor with extended half-life (24–48+ hours vs. ~minutes for native MGF). Inject post-workout; cycle to prevent receptor downregulation. Research use only.

name: PEG-MGF
notes: PEGylated Mechano Growth Factor. Extended half-life (~48-72 hours) vs MGF. Promotes muscle repair, satellite cell activation, and hypertrophy. Administer post-workout. Cycle 4-6 weeks. Lower frequency than non-PEG MGF.

name: PNC-27
dosage: Per protocol (research-stage; no established human dose)
notes: 30 mg vial. Selective anticancer peptide studied for its ability to induce apoptosis in cancer cell membranes. Very limited human data. 30 mg vial per peptidedosages.com directory. Research use only.

name: PT-141
frequency: On-demand (≥45 minutes before activity); approximately 1 dose per week maximum
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL → ~3.33 mg/mL. Bremelanotide; melanocortin MC3R/MC4R agonist for sexual dysfunction. Short-term room-temperature stability (~3 weeks if needed). Research use only.

name: Pinealon
frequency: Once daily (morning or early afternoon preferred)
notes: 20 mg vial. Standard cycles 10–20 days; may extend to 28 days. Neuroprotective tripeptide (Glu-Asp-Arg) from the pineal gland bioregulator class; studied for cognitive and antioxidant effects. May cause slight initial sedation. Research use only.

name: Pramlintide
notes: FDA-approved amylin analogue for Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes. T1D: 15-60 mcg before meals; T2D: 60-120 mcg before meals. Start low and titrate. Must reduce insulin dose by 50% when starting. Side effect: nausea.

name: Pramlintide (Symlin)
dosage: Type 1 DM: 15 mcg before meals, titrate to 30-60 mcg; Type 2 DM: 60 mcg before meals, titrate to 120 mcg
notes: Amylin analogue. Reduce insulin dose by 50% when initiating. Do not mix with insulin in same syringe. Inject at separate site from insulin. Nausea common initially; titrate slowly. Monitor glucose closely.

name: Prostamax
route: Subcutaneous injection or oral (capsule formulations exist)
notes: 20 mg vial. Bovine prostatic tissue-derived bioregulatory peptide. Repeat courses every 3–6 months. PSA monitoring recommended. No reconstitution required for some pre-measured formulations. Research use only.

name: Retatrutide
notes: Gradual escalation over 8–12 weeks; phase 2 trials showed up to 24% weight loss at 12 mg/week. Multiple vial sizes (5, 10, 20, 30 mg). Triple GLP-1/GIP/glucagon receptor agonist. Investigational compound. Research use only.

name: SLU-PP-332
frequency: Once daily (split into two divided doses in murine studies)
notes: 5 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL → 1.67 mg/mL. Pan-ERR agonist (ERRα/β/γ) exercise mimetic; evidence base is preclinical only (mouse models). No human clinical trials. Not a peptide—small organic molecule. Research use only.

name: SNAP-8
route: Subcutaneous injection (also used topically in cosmetic formulations)
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted to 3.33 mg/mL. Octapeptide analog of SNAP-25 studied for wrinkle reduction by inhibiting catecholamine release at neuromuscular junctions. Research use only.

name: SS-31
notes: Mitochondria-targeted antioxidant peptide (Elamipretide). Research doses range 1-5 mg/day SC. Used for mitochondrial dysfunction, heart failure, and anti-aging. Mostly research-stage; off-label compounded use.

name: Selank
route: Subcutaneous injection (also used intranasally in published clinical research)
notes: 5 mg or 10 mg vials; reconstituted to 1.67 mg/mL (5 mg) or 3.33 mg/mL (10 mg) with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water. Synthetic tuftsin heptapeptide analog with anxiolytic effects comparable to benzodiazepines in Russian trials, without sedation. Research use only.

name: Semaglutide
dosage: Starting dose 0.25 mg, titrating up to 1-2.4 mg
notes: Begin at 0.25 mg/week for 4 weeks, increase to 0.5 mg for 4 weeks, then 1 mg. For weight loss, may titrate to 1.7 mg then 2.4 mg. GI side effects common during titration. Rotate injection sites.

name: Semax
dosage: 200–500 mcg (5 mg vial) or 300–800 mcg (10 mg vial)
notes: 5 mg vial: 3.0 mL → 1.67 mg/mL; 10 mg vial: 3.0 mL → 3.33 mg/mL. Titrate from 200–300 mcg up to 400–500 mcg over 5–12 weeks. ACTH(4-10) heptapeptide analog; studied for cognitive enhancement, cerebrovascular recovery, optic neuropathy. Research use only.

name: Sermorelin
notes: GHRH analogue that stimulates natural GH release. Half-life is short (~10-20 min). Effects develop gradually over weeks. Often used for anti-aging and body composition. Cycle 3-6 months.

name: Survodutide
notes: 10 mg vial reconstituted with 2.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields 5 mg/mL. Gradual titration over 10–12 weeks. Dual GLP-1/glucagon receptor agonist (BI 456906). Phase 2 trials: 12.5–14.9% weight loss over 46 weeks. Research use only.

name: TB-4 Frag (Thymosin Beta-4 Fragment)
frequency: Once daily or every other day
notes: Active fragment of TB-4 (aa 17-23). Used for tissue repair and anti-inflammatory effects. Typical cycle 4-6 weeks. Lower cost alternative to full TB-4. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water.

name: TB-500
frequency: Twice weekly for 4-6 weeks (loading), then once weekly or biweekly (maintenance)
notes: Often stacked with BPC-157 for synergistic healing. Loading phase: 4-6 weeks at higher frequency. Maintenance: 2-2.5 mg every 1-2 weeks. Systemic distribution makes injection site less critical.

name: Teriparatide
notes: FDA-approved recombinant PTH(1-34) for osteoporosis. Standard dose: 20 mcg/day SC. Maximum treatment duration: 2 years lifetime due to osteosarcoma risk (seen in rats). Inject into thigh or abdomen. Increases bone density and reduces fracture risk.

name: Teriparatide (PTH 1-34)
notes: FDA-approved for osteoporosis at 20 mcg/day. Maximum treatment duration 2 years due to osteosarcoma risk in animal studies. Rotate injection sites. Monitor calcium and serum PTH. Discontinue if hypercalcemia develops.

name: Tesamorelin
notes: FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy at 2 mg/day. Off-label use for visceral fat reduction and cognitive benefits. Stable GHRH analogue. Cycle 3-6 months with reassessment.

name: Testagen
notes: 20 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~6.67 mg/mL. Khavinson tetrapeptide (Lys-Glu-Asp-Gly) anterior pituitary bioregulator. No human clinical trials exist. Research use only.

name: Thymalin
notes: Thymic peptide bioregulator. Typical protocol: 5-10 mg/day IM for 10 consecutive days, 1-2x per year. Immune modulation and thymic restoration. Part of Khavinson peptide series. Often combined with Epithalon for anti-aging protocols.

name: Thymogen (Glutamyl-Tryptophan)
notes: Dipeptide derived from thymosin alpha-1. Immunomodulatory effects. Nasal drops also used at 0.025% solution. Cycles typically repeated 2-4 times per year.

name: Thymosin Alpha-1
notes: Immune modulator. FDA-approved in some countries for hepatitis and immune disorders. Typical protocol: 1.6 mg SC twice weekly for 6-12 weeks. Used for chronic infections, cancer support, and immune enhancement. Well-tolerated.

name: Thymosin Beta-4
notes: Same active component as TB-500 (primary sequence fragment). Used for healing, inflammation, and cardiac protection. Standard protocol: 1-2 mg 2-3x/week for 4-6 weeks loading, then maintenance 1-2 mg/week.

name: Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500)
dosage: Loading phase: 2-2.5 mg twice weekly for 4-6 weeks; Maintenance: 1-2 mg twice monthly
frequency: Twice weekly during loading; twice monthly for maintenance
notes: Promotes wound healing, reduces inflammation, improves flexibility and recovery. Some protocols use 5 mg/week split into two doses. Systemic distribution allows injection away from injury site.

name: Thymulin (Facteur Thymique Serique)
notes: Zinc-dependent thymic hormone. Requires adequate zinc for activity. Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory. Research doses vary widely. Zinc supplementation may enhance efficacy.

name: Tirzepatide
dosage: Starting dose 2.5 mg, titrating up to 5-15 mg
notes: Begin at 2.5 mg/week for 4 weeks, increase by 2.5 mg increments every 4 weeks as tolerated. Max dose 15 mg/week. Dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist. Superior weight loss outcomes compared to semaglutide in trials.

name: Triptorelin
frequency: Single injection for PCT; monthly or quarterly for clinical use
notes: GnRH agonist. Single low-dose 0.1 mg injection used in PCT for HPTA restart. Clinical depot formulations (3.75 mg monthly) used for prostate cancer, endometriosis. PCT use: one-time injection. Paradoxically stimulates LH/FSH at low dose.

name: VIP
notes: Vasoactive intestinal peptide. Used for CIRS (Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome), pulmonary hypertension, and neuroinflammation. Typical dose: 50 mcg intranasal 4x/day in clinical protocols (Shoemaker protocol). Anti-inflammatory properties.

name: VIP (Vasoactive Intestinal Peptide)
notes: Used in CIRS (Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome) protocols per Shoemaker protocol. Anti-inflammatory neuropeptide. Must rule out MARCoNS before use. Monitor VIP levels pre and post treatment.

name: Vesugen
notes: 20 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~6.67 mg/mL. Khavinson tripeptide (Lys-Glu-Asp / KED) vascular bioregulator. Studied for endothelial cell proliferation and SIRT-1 modulation. Research use only.

name: Vilon
frequency: Once daily for 5 consecutive days per 4-week cycle (pulsed protocol)
notes: 20 mg vial reconstituted with 3.0 mL bacteriostatic water yields ~6.67 mg/mL. Khavinson immunoregulatory dipeptide (Lys-Glu). Pulsed 5-days-on protocol per cycle. Studied for lymphocyte modulation and longevity biomarkers. Research use only.
